vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-704: Rally Features missing action buttons in kanban (Plan / See Plan / vBRIEF / Tasks)

plan.title: Rally Features missing action buttons in kanban (Plan / See Plan / vBRIEF / Tasks)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Rally Features (PortfolioItem/Feature) render via FeatureCard in the kanban with no action bar, so there is no UI affordance to trigger feature-level planning, view the vBRIEF, or view tasks. The backend supports `pan work plan <feature-id>` already; the dashboard is the gap.
narratives.Proposal: Extract the existing Plan/vBRIEF/Tasks chips from IssueCard into reusable components, then render them on FeatureCard at the bottom of the header row (visible collapsed and expanded). Wire onPlan/onViewVBrief/onViewBeads props through the hierarchical render path. No Start Agent chip — work happens per-story. CompactChildCard unchanged.

items.title: Extract PlanChip / VBriefChip / TasksChip into reusable components

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/kanban/PlanningChips.tsx` exporting three components: PlanChip, VBriefChip, TasksChip. Each takes `{ issue, onPlan | onViewVBrief | onViewBeads }` and owns its own `useQuery(['planning-state', issue.identifier])` with the existing refetchInterval: 30000 / staleTime: 15000 settings. TasksChip also owns the `generate-tasks` mutation and click logic. The chips must render pixel-identical JSX/styling to the current inline chips in IssueCard. Then update IssueCard to consume the extracted components, removing its inline `planChip`/`vbriefChip`/`tasksChip` JSX but keeping its own `planningStateQuery` (React Query dedupes by key so no extra network cost) since IssueCard still needs `beadsCount` for the Start Agent gate.

subItems.title: New file PlanningChips.tsx exports PlanChip, VBriefChip, TasksChip with identical styling/behavior to the current inline chips
Each chip hosts its own useQuery(['planning-state', issue.identifier]) with refetchInterval: 30000 and staleTime: 15000
TasksChip owns the generate-tasks mutation and shows 'Generate Tasks' (red/destructive) when hasPlan && beadsCount === 0
IssueCard is refactored to consume the extracted components; its existing Plan/vBRIEF/Tasks rendering in backlog/todo/in_progress/done action bars remains visually identical
Existing KanbanBoard tests (if any) still pass; typecheck and lint pass

items.title: Add Plan / vBRIEF / Tasks action bar to FeatureCard

narrative.Action: Add `onPlan`, `onViewVBrief`, `onViewBeads`, and `agents` props to FeatureCard. Render a new action bar at the bottom of the clickable header `<div>` in FeatureCard (after the progress bar, before the expanded children region) containing `<PlanChip>`, `<VBriefChip>`, `<TasksChip>` from the extracted components. Each chip's onClick must call e.stopPropagation() so the click does not toggle expand/collapse. PlanChip must support the existing `isPlanningActive` variant from IssueCard — when a planning agent is running for the feature (derived from the `agents` prop by matching `agent.issueId === feature.identifier && agent.agentPhase === 'planning' && agent.status !== 'dead'`), render the pulsing Eye 'Watch Planning' button in place of the Plan chip, identical to IssueCard lines 2711-2722. Update the hierarchical render path in IssueColumn (`renderIssueCard`) to pass `onPlan={() => onPlan(feature)}`, `onViewVBrief={onViewVBrief ? (i) => onViewVBrief(i) : undefined}`, `onViewBeads={(i) => onViewBeads(i)}`, and `agents={agents}` through to the `<FeatureCard>` at KanbanBoard.tsx:1675. CompactChildCard must remain unchanged.

subItems.title: FeatureCard accepts onPlan, onViewVBrief, onViewBeads props
FeatureCard renders an action bar containing PlanChip, VBriefChip, TasksChip in its header (visible when the feature is collapsed AND expanded)
Rally Features in backlog, todo, and in_progress columns show all three chips on the kanban
Clicking Plan opens PlanDialog targeting the Feature identifier via onPlan(feature)
Clicking vBRIEF invokes onViewVBrief(feature) and opens the existing VBriefViewer
Clicking Tasks invokes onViewBeads(feature) (or generate-tasks when hasPlan && beadsCount === 0)
'See Plan' (green) appears on FeatureCard once a feature-level vBRIEF exists (hasPlan === true)
Chip clicks do not toggle the feature's expand/collapse state (stopPropagation verified)
No Start Agent button on FeatureCard
CompactChildCard is unchanged (no new buttons, no new props)

When a planning agent is running for the feature (isPlanningActive), the Plan chip is replaced by a pulsing Eye 'Watch Planning' button identical to IssueCard's isPlanningActive variant; clicking it invokes onPlan(feature)
FeatureCard receives an `agents` prop from IssueColumn used to derive isPlanningActive for the feature

items.title: Add RTL component test for FeatureCard action bar

narrative.Action: Add a Vitest + React Testing Library test (colocated with the existing KanbanBoard frontend tests, or as a new `FeatureCard.test.tsx`) that renders FeatureCard wrapped in a QueryClientProvider, mocks the /api/issues/:id/planning-state response via MSW or a fetch stub, and asserts the rendered chips + click behavior. Note: FeatureCard is currently not exported from KanbanBoard.tsx — export it (named export) as part of this bead so the test can import it directly.

subItems.title: Test renders FeatureCard with Plan, vBRIEF, Tasks chips visible
Test verifies 'See Plan' (green) appears when planning-state returns hasPlan: true
Test verifies clicking Plan calls onPlan with the feature
Test verifies clicking vBRIEF calls onViewVBrief with the feature
Test verifies clicking Tasks calls onViewBeads with the feature (when beadsCount > 0)
Test verifies chip clicks do NOT call onToggle (stopPropagation)
Test passes under `npm test` (frontend vitest)
